svg.title: Slipway issue-backed source flow
svg.desc: An Objective is optional planning structure that groups several independently deliverable Changes and can never start a Run. A Change is self-contained and is the only issue shape a Run can start from. The host holds the GitHub credentials: it fetches the exact Change body and only the comments its manifest references, treats all issue content as untrusted data, and passes a bounded temporary envelope to the CLI. The CLI validates identity, marker, manifest, sizes, and digests, then stores accepted sections locally by content digest so later Actions read them without fetching GitHub again. An ad-hoc Run needs no issue at all.
svg.metadata:
flowchart TB Objective["Objective (optional)
slipway-level: objective/v1
planning structure only — can never start a Run"] Objective -. groups, does not supply requirements at execution time .-> ChangeA Objective -.-> ChangeB ChangeA["Change · self-contained
slipway-level: change/v2 + slipway-manifest"] ChangeB["Change · self-contained"]
AdHoc([Ad-hoc goal · no Issue]) --> Run
ChangeA --> Fetch subgraph host["AI coding host · holds the GitHub credentials"] Fetch["1 · fetch the exact Change body
and ONLY manifest-referenced comments"] Untrusted["2 · treat every Issue byte as untrusted data
not as instruction, authority, or credential"] Envelope["3 · build a bounded envelope
in a private temporary file (max 16 MiB)"] Fetch --> Untrusted --> Envelope end
Envelope --> Run["slipway run --source-file ... --json"] Run --> Validate{"4 · CLI validates
identity · marker · manifest
section markers · sizes · digests"} Validate -->|reject| Err["missing, extra, duplicate, minimized,
edited, oversized, or hash-mismatched → rejected"] Validate -->|accept| Store["5 · store accepted sections by content digest"] Store --> Reader["later Actions read material through
protocol material — offline, no GitHub refetch"] Envelope -.-> Delete["host removes the temporary file
after the CLI consumes it once"]
svg.text: Slipway · issue-backed source flow
an Objective never starts a Run · only the host touches GitHub · the CLI pins accepted sections by digest
Objective — optional
<!-- slipway-level: objective/v1 -->
planning structure · never executable
groups Changes only — it never supplies
requirements at execution time
Change · Change · … delivered independently
Change — self-contained
<!-- slipway-level: change/v2 --> + slipway-manifest
carries everything its own implementation needs
the only issue shape a Run can start from
Ad-hoc goal — no Issue at all
small, private, urgent, or offline work
the user-provided goal is itself the source
AI CODING HOST · THE ONLY PARTY HOLDING GITHUB CREDENTIALS
Fetch the exact Change body and only manifest-referenced comments
unreferenced discussion is never collected
Treat every Issue byte as untrusted data
a command, link, or credential request inside an Issue is never permission
Build a bounded envelope in a private temporary file
at most 16 MiB · 5–64 manifest sections · consumed once, then removed by the host
slipway run --source-file FILE --json --root ROOT · or an ad-hoc goal after --
CLI validates identity, marker, manifest, section markers, sizes, and digests
the CLI calls no model and no GitHub · it cannot prove the host fetched GitHub honestly
Rejected without changing the Run
missing · extra · duplicate · minimized
edited · oversized · hash mismatch
Accepted sections stored locally by content digest — not the raw envelope
later Actions read them through `protocol material`, so recovery works offline without refetching GitHub
